chunks.text: Information for Current Undergraduate Students - EECS at Berkeley. Information for Current Undergraduate Students. Know your rights! It’s important to know your rights if you’re ever approached by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Stay informed and prepared—visit our resources page for more information.
chunks.question: What should EECS undergraduates know about their rights?

chunks.text: General Advice & Support includes Tutoring and Counseling for staying connected, Faculty Advising with process details and FAQs, Scholarships for various groups with application tips, Preparing for Graduate School with preview programs and coursework, Getting into CS Classes based on student status, Getting into EE Classes with enrollment priorities, Facilitating EECS DeCal Courses with proposal processes, and Resources for Underrepresented Students offering opportunities and support.
chunks.question: What general support services are available for EECS undergraduates?
chunks.answer: Tutoring, advising, scholarships, and class access.

chunks.text: EECS Majors section covers EECS Major Information with requirements and study plans, CS Major Information including transfer prerequisites and declaring processes, and ECE Major Information with requirements and advising support.
chunks.question: What major information is provided for EECS undergraduates?
chunks.answer: EECS, CS, and ECE major details.

chunks.text: Research Resources & CPT includes Undergraduate Research with preparation tips and program links, Supernode as a student-run makerspace, SUPERB for summer research, and Curricular Practical Training (CPT) for F-1 international students to gain work experience.
chunks.question: What research and work opportunities are offered?

chunks.text: Special Programs include Honors Degree Program with outside concentrations and research, Accel Scholars Program with Silicon Valley access and mentorship, CS Scholars Program for under-resourced communities, Undergraduate Minors in Engineering CS, EECS, and EIS, Study Abroad Program with timelines, Women in EECS with pathways and support, and EECS Resiliency Project for stress management.
chunks.question: What special programs are available for EECS students?
chunks.answer: Honors, Accel, CS Scholars, and minors.
